chapter_title.ar: باب مَنْ نَامَ عَنِ الصَّلَاةِ أَوْ سَهَا عَنْهَا
chapter_title.en: Chapter 12 – The one who sleeps through the Salāt, or forgets about it

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/64)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/64)
text: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ نَسِيَ الظُّهْرَ حَتَّى غَرَبَتِ الشَّمْسُ وَ قَدْ كَانَ صَلَّى الْعَصْرَ فَقَالَ كَانَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) أَوْ كَانَ أَبِي ( عليه السلام ) يَقُولُ إِنْ أَمْكَنَهُ أَنْ يُصَلِّيَهَا قَبْلَ أَنْ يَفُوتَهُ الْمَغْرِبُ بَدَأَ بِهَا وَ إِلَّا صَلَّى الْمَغْرِبَ ثُمَّ صَلَّاهَا .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Al Fazl Bin ShAzan, from Safwan Bin Yahya,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup>, said, ‘I asked him<sup>asws</sup> about a man who forgot Al-Zohr until the sun set, and he had prayed Al-Asr. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup>’, or said: ‘My<sup>asws</sup> father<sup>asws</sup> was saying that if you are able to pray it before missing out on Al-Maghrib, begin with it, or else pray Al-Maghrib, then pray it (Al-Zohr)’.
en.sarwar: 6. Muhammad ibn ’Isma‘il has narrated from al-Fadl ibn Shadhan from Safwan ibn Yahya who has said the following: “I once asked abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who forgets al-Dhuhr until it is sunset and has performed al-‘Asr. He (the Imam) said, ‘Abu Ja’far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, or my father would say, “If he can perform Qada’ (compensatory prayer) for it before the time for al-Maghrib ends, he must do so, otherwise, he performs al-Maghrib then Qada’ (compensatory prayer) for that which is missed.’”
